title: Vegetable Cookery
content:
CLASSES

Fresh: Crisp, firm, fully matured for characteristic flavor. Wash thoroughly. Store cool until needed. Many (turnips, carrots, beets, tomatoes, lettuce, watercress) need not be cooked.

Dried: Firm, free from blemishes. Restore water content by soaking 1 hour to overnight, then cooking slowly in soaking water. Or cover with cold water and cook slowly (longer time required).

Canned: Firm, free from blemishes. Check for reasonable proportion of liquid to solid. Heat thoroughly before using. Do not use if container bulges.

PREPARATION

Freshening: If wilted, wash, cover with cold water, allow to stand until plump. Or sprinkle, place in refrigerator pan, cover.

Washing: Wash just before cooking/cutting. Do not soak after paring/slicing (nutrient loss). If tend to brown, sprinkle with lemon juice or ascorbic acid.

Uniform sizing: Cut pieces uniform size for even cooking.

Slicing: Cut round vegetables in half first; place flat side down. Hold with claw grip; use rocking motion with knife, pivoting on tip.

French cuts: Brunoise ⅛ inch, Salpicon ¼ inch, Macedoine ⅜ inch, Jardiniere/Julienne 2-3 inches long by ⅛ inch, Pearls round, Olivette ¼ inch, Noisette ½ inch, Parisienne 1 inch.
